Transcrição Completa: 27 Day 27

 

[00:00] Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to day number 27. We're in a new environment. We got some friendly faces behind us here, but that's not going to distract you, deviate you, or lower your discipline from hitting your 100 per day.

 

[00:14] Once again, you should be much higher than that. You should now need to understand a few things. Number one, let's account for the fact you're probably having to start delivering for some of your clients.

 

[00:26] It's important that you're doing team building. It's important that you're literally in real time expanding your operational capacity. That way you have the ability in real time to grow your business while you're making sales for your business.

 

[00:41] And every single thing that you're starting to do, keep in mind, you want to use hats for these things. So you want to be able to take what it is you do, write it down for what you do, the responsibilities of what you do.

 

[00:53] There's entire video series on hats. Every single thing that you don't know how to do, once again, is in this entire program. It's limited by your ability to go search the videos that you probably just haven't watched yet.

 

[01:06] Every single answer literally is within this entire program for you. And if it's not, I make a video for it because that's how I hold myself accountable to your success.

 

[01:16] Now, listen, I want you to use assets. And one of the downloadable assets inside of this very program is what I call the lead graph.

 

[01:24] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Lead Graph - Content Consumption Matrix"

 

NÚMEROS: 50% attrition rate (taxa de desistência em webinars)

 

VISUAL: Gráfico quadrante mostrando quatro categorias de conteúdo organizadas por dois eixos: "Hard to Consume vs Easy to Consume" (vertical) e "Low Desire vs High Desire" (horizontal). Quadrante superior esquerdo: "Webinar/Video Series" (Hard to Consume, High Desire). Quadrante superior direito: "PDF Download" (Easy to Consume, High Desire). Quadrante inferior esquerdo: "Sales Calls" (Hard to Consume, Low Desire). Quadrante inferior direito: "Ebooks" (Easy to Consume, Low Desire).

 

[01:26] Now, this is a pretty cool pitch and I think it's going to be fun for you to look at this and see it from a perspective where if I'm using Loom, like use loom.com, you can use the screen capture tool, use the webcam in the bottom corner there.

 

[01:40] Just a good example of what you can do. So, my screen, I'm going to show you guys what's up. So this is downloadable once again for you.

 

[01:48] In the top left here, you'll see in this webinar video series box, it says hard to consume and high desire. So that means that a webinar or video series, most people want to watch these things.

 

[02:02] Most people register. I run webinars myself. You'll typically get attrition rate, meaning people who sign up and don't register about 50%.

 

[02:11] I also work with the world's largest personality brands who run a lot of info products and webinars themselves to get people there. And a lot of these things are getting people signing up. About half the people just don't watch.

 

[02:23] So, check that out. That means it's of high desire, yet it's hard to consume. The box next to that that's technically the best is a PDF download or some kind of quick downloadable asset.

 

[02:36] A lot of people will use this in any kind of space that you'll see online where they'll now do the videos of screen captures showing whatever you can download. You know, high desire, easy to consume. People love that.

 

[02:49] Now, hard to consume and low desire are sales calls and easy to consume and low desire are ebooks.

 

[02:57] Now, when you're going through this and you now understand what this is, this is essentially how you want to do this video. So, you want to open up once again Loom and this is what you want to say.

 

[03:08] "Hey, what's going on, Bob? So, wanted to reach out and I want to show you this fun lead graph. This shows that, you know, low desire and easy to consume lead generating mechanisms that we can put out on the internet right now."

 

[03:23] "We can do these simple pages we've talked about. I know we've talked about a few. I know I've shown you a few. I know I've given you a few examples at this point, but hear me out, Bob."

 

[03:32] "Ebooks, you people, you know, they're easy to consume. People don't want them though. Sales calls, they're hard to consume and people don't want them."

 

[03:40] "What is technically the best are PDF downloads, meaning people will want them. They're high desire and they're easy to consume. I'm sure you maybe have downloaded a few PDFs. Now, rarely do we actually read these, Bob."

 

[03:53] "But check this out. Webinars and video series are technically the highest of desires, yet the hardest to consume. But Bob, hear me out."

 

[04:03] "Did you know that Facebook ad compliance technically doesn't like webinars? It lowers your reach, increases your cost per click."

 

[04:12] "Did you know that Facebook doesn't technically like taking people off their website unless it is for a direct purchase? They love e-commerce sites."

 

[04:21] "They're starting to hate people in any kind of space that is not selling something directly after you take them off Facebook. They don't do autoplay videos anymore."

 

[04:31] "They don't give you the capacity to do those exit popups where you could collect a lead. Maybe you don't do any of that stuff. Maybe you just have a slow page load speed."

 

[04:40] "Whatever it is, if you're giving any of these things away, even if you're not, dude, you're getting affected if that page isn't loading quickly."

 

[04:48] "So, I show you this whole graph because this is something that we used to use for clients that gave an example of different desires and different levels of if they were easy or hard to consume for things that people would, you know, commonly hear about within different industries that they could use in their business, especially service businesses in order to generate leads online."

 

[05:10] "Now, check this out, Bob. I show you this because straight up with all that Facebook ad policy, you got to have a new marketing plan."

 

[05:18] "You got to have the ability to take these steps out of it and have an entire retargeting model of video sequences and of aggressive ad campaigns and of things that our staff, our team, we all have the capability to do."

 

[05:32] "Or if it's just you, you can mention that you're a digital marketer here and that's where you can talk about this specific skill or whatever skill you're trying to sell them on. You don't have to just use ads."

 

[05:43] "Now, in this specific example, Bob, once again, just wanted to give you a visual. I'll shoot this over via email. Should you have any questions, let me know."

 

[05:52] "You know, if you don't, I'm looking forward to a conversation on what we can do to get your entire system online in Facebook ad compliance. That way, we can keep doing Facebook ads."

 

[06:03] "And that way, just in case you have any of these out there, I wanted to give you a visual on where they're kind of ranked at. This all comes from data."

 

[06:11] "Should you have any questions, reach out to me, give me a time if we can chat tomorrow, you know, morning, afternoon, whatever works best for you, Bob. Looking forward to chatting and I will talk soon."

 

[06:22] And in simple videos like this upon you in real time, taking data that's in the DMM and sending it out, you in real time taking the data that's in the DMM and sending it out is your ability in real time to close deals a little faster.

 

[06:37] Because keep in mind 35% of people are visual. 40% of people in life are moved on a kinesthetic level, meaning emotionally or they have to kind of feel that they want to take an action of some kind.

 

[06:51] Go out, hit your numbers today. Do not fail in your ability to discipline yourself to close some deals. You're on day 27. You're almost done with the 30-day sales challenge.

 

[07:02] Round it up. Keep strong. Keep it moving. And more importantly, be an agency owner. Deliver operationally. Build out your staff. Keep at it. Keep your numbers up.

 

[07:13] At this point, your biggest absolutely only excuse at this point for why you might be bitching about not hitting your hundred is because "Jeremy, now I have to deliver and I have sales meetings. I have appointments and the emails too long."

 

[07:28] "And where am I even getting all these emails from?" Dude, there's two billion people on Facebook alone. You're trying to tell me you can't take the time to hire a VA and go out and get emails in real time.

 

[07:40] You're telling me that you're limited by your own creative thinking to justify to yourself that you literally don't have enough emails and you're only on day 27.

 

[07:50] You have sent 2700 emails to this point. If you've hit 100 per day and you want to complain about the fact that you don't have enough emails or that you don't have the capacity to go out and deliver and build your business and all this other stuff, you're on the 30-day sales challenge.

 

[08:08] I told you at the beginning of this, you're hitting 100 per day. You don't have any excuses to make. You're still going to be able to do it.

 

[08:16] Now, you want to know a pro tip and a little hack on how you have the capability to do that? Take your skills, duplicate them into a salesperson.

 

[08:25] Hire an entire team to go out and do these things that you just learned to do over the last 27 days. More importantly, go hit your hundred.

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

Dia 27 do desafio de 30 dias

Meta diária: 100 emails

Total acumulado esperado: 2.700 emails enviados

Taxa de desistência em webinars: 50%

35% das pessoas são visuais

40% das pessoas são movidas por fatores cinestésicos/emocionais

2 bilhões de pessoas no Facebook

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Lead Graph Matrix: Framework de classificação de conteúdo por desejo e facilidade de consumo

Loom Videos: Uso de screen capture com webcam para pitches personalizados

Sistema de Hats: Documentação de responsabilidades e funções

Retargeting Model: Sequências de vídeo e campanhas agressivas de anúncios

Facebook Ad Compliance: Adaptação às novas políticas da plataforma

Team Building: Expansão operacional em tempo real

VA Hiring: Contratação de assistentes virtuais para sourcing de emails

Duplicação de Skills: Transferência de habilidades para equipe de vendas

 

ESTRUTURA:

Quadrante Superior Direito (Melhor): PDF Downloads - High Desire, Easy to Consume

Quadrante Superior Esquerdo: Webinars/Video Series - High Desire, Hard to Consume

Quadrante Inferior Direito: Ebooks - Low Desire, Easy to Consume

Quadrante Inferior Esquerdo (Pior): Sales Calls - Low Desire, Hard to Consume

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Não mencionados especificamente neste vídeo

 

POLÍTICAS E COMPLIANCE:

Facebook reduz alcance e aumenta CPC para webinars

Facebook favorece e-commerce sobre lead generation

Fim de autoplay videos

Remoção de exit popups

Velocidade de carregamento de página afeta performance

 

ENTREGÁVEIS DO PITCH:

Email de follow-up com visual do Lead Graph

Proposta de novo plano de marketing

Sistema completo em conformidade com Facebook Ads

 

DURAÇÃO: ~8:30 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo de treinamento/coaching direto com demonstração de ferramenta (Lead Graph)
